entries.definition:
ṣ–r–r freezing cold, frost; (sound) screeching, grating, creaking; (of a buzzard) to cry; to tie up, to purse, to constrict; to persist. Of this root, three forms occur six times in the Qurʾan: أَصَرَّ aṣarra four times; صِرّ ṣirr once and صَرَّة ṣarratin once.
أَصَرَّ aṣarra [v. IV, intrans.] to insist, persist (45:8) يَسْمَعُ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ تُتْلَى عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ يُصِرُّ مُسْتَكْبِرًا who hears God’s revelations being recited to him, yet persists in his arrogance.

صِرّ ṣirr [n.] extreme coldness (also extreme heat) (3:117) كَمَثَلِ رِيحٍ فِيهَا صِرٌّ أَصَابَتْ حَرْثَ قَوْمٍ ظَلَمُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ فَأَهْلَكَتْهُ like a wind full of biting cold that strikes and destroys the harvest of a people who have wronged themselves.

صَرَّة ṣarratin [v. n.] loud cry, great commotion (51:29) فَأَقْبَلَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ فِي صَرَّةٍ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ then his wife entered with a loud cry, struck her [own] face and said, ‘[I am only] a barren old woman!’
